read-tracked-and-untracked-state.label: Read the Community Project State
read-tracked-and-untracked-state.summary: Carry the same community-project files from untracked state into one staged snapshot and inspect the proposal.

sketch1.title: Short Status Is a Compact Map

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.title: Read the Initial Repository State
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.prompt: The prepared `community-project/` repository has no commits. Its five plain-text project files are untracked. Ask Git for the full repository report, read how it classifies the files, and leave the state unchanged.
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.hint: Use the full status report as a read-only description of the repository.

help.concept: Untracked files exist in the working tree but are not yet part of a proposed or committed snapshot.
help.hint_1: The report should say there are no commits yet.
help.hint_2: The supplied project files should appear as untracked.

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.

zoeskoul_setup_sh.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- community-project
mkdir -p -- community-project
git -C community-project init -q -b main
git -C community-project config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C community-project config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s '# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
' > community-project/README.md
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community schedule

Saturday cleanup: 10:00 AM
' > community-project/schedule.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Community contacts

Help desk: 555-0100
' > community-project/contacts.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project
printf %s 'Old planning notes

Spring flyer draft.
' > community-project/old-notes.txt
mkdir -p -- community-project/generated
printf %s 'Generated summary

Created automatically from project notes.
' > community-project/generated/summary.txt

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.instructions: The prepared `community-project/` repository has no commits. Its five plain-text project files are untracked. Ask Git for the full repository report, read how it classifies the files, and leave the state unchanged.

0.message: That action can discard or rewrite history. Use the safe, layer-specific workflow taught in this course.
Did you ask Git for the full report without changing the repository?

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.title: Stage the First Proposed Snapshot
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.prompt: The same five files remain untracked: `README.md`, `schedule.txt`, `contacts.txt`, `old-notes.txt`, and `generated/summary.txt`. Stage exactly those files, then use short status to confirm they moved into the staging area.
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.hint: Name the five supplied files explicitly, then read their compact staged state.

help.concept: git add copies selected working-tree versions into the staging area; short status then shows the proposed snapshot in its left column.
help.hint_1: Use only the five paths named in the prompt.
help.hint_2: Every supplied file should appear as a staged addition.

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.starterCode:
# Community Project

Shared schedules and contact notes.
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.solutionCode:
git add README.md schedule.txt contacts.txt old-notes.txt generated/summary.txt
git status --short

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.instructions: The same five files remain untracked: `README.md`, `schedule.txt`, `contacts.txt`, `old-notes.txt`, and `generated/summary.txt`. Stage exactly those files, then use short status to confirm they moved into the staging area.

1.message: Did you inspect their compact staged state?

try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.title: Review the Staged File Summary
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.prompt: The five files are staged and no commit exists yet. Inspect the staged summary so you can verify the proposed snapshot without recording it.
try_read_tracked_and_untracked_state_ske.hint: Read the staged summary and leave the proposal unchanged.

help.concept: git diff --staged --stat summarizes the files already proposed for the next commit.
help.hint_2: The staged state must remain ready for the next topic.

single-choice-2.title: Understanding git status --short
single-choice-2.prompt: What does the `??` symbol indicate in the output of `git status --short`?
single-choice-2.hint: Focus on what the command or concept observes or changes at this point in the workflow.

help.concept: Read full and short status reports to tell untracked files, unstaged edits, staged work, and clean state apart.
help.hint_1: Remove options that act on a different repository layer than the one named in the question.
help.hint_2: Choose the answer that remains accurate even when other unrelated files exist.

multi-choice-1.title: Read the Two Columns
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which statements about short status are correct? Select all that apply.
multi-choice-1.hint: Judge each option independently; choose only the statements that stay true in this exact repository state.

help.concept: Read full and short status reports to tell untracked files, unstaged edits, staged work, and clean state apart.
help.hint_1: A partly true option is still wrong if it adds an effect Git does not guarantee.
help.hint_2: Use the layer named in the prompt—working tree, staging area, or history—to test each choice.

options.a: The left column describes staged changes.
options.b: The right column describes working-tree changes.
options.d: Every listed path has already been committed.

fill-blank-choice-1.title: Unstaged Modification Column
fill-blank-choice-1.prompt: In short status, an unstaged tracked-file edit uses the letter `[blank1]` in the right column.
fill-blank-choice-1.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Read full and short status reports to tell untracked files, unstaged edits, staged work, and clean state apart.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

fill-blank-choice-1.template: An unstaged tracked-file edit uses `[blank1]` in the right column.

fill-blank-choice-2.title: Untracked File Marker
fill-blank-choice-2.prompt: In short status, a file Git has never tracked begins with `[blank1]`.
fill-blank-choice-2.hint: Choose the term or symbol that matches the exact state described, not a nearby Git concept.

help.concept: Read full and short status reports to tell untracked files, unstaged edits, staged work, and clean state apart.
help.hint_1: Pay attention to whether the prompt describes an untracked path, a working-tree edit, staged work, or history.
help.hint_2: Read spaces, punctuation, and status columns as meaningful when the question shows Git output.

fill-blank-choice-2.template: A file Git has never tracked begins with `[blank1]`.

sketch0.title: Status Describes the Current Layers
sketch0.bodyMarkdown: `git status` compares the working tree, staging area, and current commit. Before the first commit, the five supplied files are untracked. Staging proposes them for the next snapshot without recording history yet.

sketch1.title: Short Status Is a Compact Map
sketch1.bodyMarkdown: `git status --short` uses two positions: the left describes the staging area and the right describes the working tree. Read each mark as a location in the workflow rather than as an isolated symbol to memorize.

sketch2.title: Inspection and Action Are Different
sketch2.bodyMarkdown: Status and diff commands only describe state. `git add` changes the staging area. The course keeps those actions separate so you can explain what moved and what stayed unchanged.
